input.prompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.content:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

<current_datetime>2026-05-13T00:59:34.678Z</current_datetime>

I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using stdio transport. They need:

1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

Let me first explore what's in the current working directory, then build the solution.

data.content: Empty directory. I'll scaffold a .NET project with the official MCP C# SDK, wire up stdio transport, add an API-calling tool, a summarization prompt, and route logs to stderr so they don't corrupt the JSON-RPC stream on stdout.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-bb61ca9edf3c4e7c98f9a3d0e622c27b && dotnet new console -n McpServer --no-restore -o . 2>&1 | tail -3

Good, packages are installed. Now let me check the SDK's API surface to understand how to properly set up tools and prompts, then create the files.

arguments.description: Find MCP SDK docs and assemblies

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "find ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.3.0 -name \"*.xml\" | head -5 && find ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.3.0 -name \"*.dll\" | head -5", "description": "Find MCP SDK docs and assemblies"}

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net11.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="10.0.8" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Http" Version="10.0.8" />
    <PackageReference Include="ModelContextProtocol" Version="1.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
<exited with exit code 0>
